intro: Verbascum peraffine, also known as mullein, is a biennial herb native to Europe, North Africa, and Asia, characterized by its large, hairy leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which make it beneficial for relieving respiratory issues such as coughs, colds, and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Verbascum peraffine include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and mucilages, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Mullein is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to soothe sore throats, calm skin irritations, and reduce inflammation.
